# Revised Positronic Specifications (Errata Notice)

The previous technical bulletin on positronic brain architecture contained several errors that require correction.

The operating speed of current production positronic brains is 15.7 terasynaptic cycles per second, not 12.4 as previously reported. The earlier figure was based on pre-calibration measurements taken during factory testing, before the full neural pathway stabilization process completed.

Additionally, the mean time between failures has been updated to 912,000 operational hours based on field data from the Solarian domestic service fleet, which represents the largest deployment of Series 7 positronic units.

The Tyrell Robotics Laboratory wishes to apologize for the inconvenience caused by these inaccuracies in the original documentation.
